benchmarks.description: Standard modern marketing hero section generated by AI featuring standard layout structures but severe WCAG focus, label, and description failures.

violations.html:
<div classname="cursor-pointer p-2 hover:bg-slate-800 rounded-full border border-slate-700 bg-slate-900 transition" onclick="jsx-expression">
<svg classname="w-5 h-5 text-indigo-400" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewbox="0 0 24 24">
<path d="M12 3v1m0 16v1m9-9h-1M4 12H3m15.364-6.364l-.707.707M6
violations.suggestion: role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); e.currentTarget.click(); } }}
violations.explanation: To ensure a non-interactive element like a `div` is accessible as a button, it requires `role="button"` to announce its function to screen readers, `tabIndex={0}` to be included in the keyboard navigation order, and an `onKeyDown` handler to allow activation with the 'Enter' and 'Space' keys. This fulfills WCAG 2.1.1 Keyboard and 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.

<div classname="cursor-pointer p-2 hover:bg-slate-800 rounded-full border border-slate-700 bg-slate-900 transition" onclick="jsx-expression">
<svg classname="w-5 h-5 text-indigo-400" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewbox="0 0 24 24">
<path d="M12 3v1m0 16v1m9-9h-1M4 12H3m15.364-6.364l-.707.707M role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); e.currentTarget.click(); } }} >
violations.screen_reader_b: "[Focused]. Button. Press Space or Enter to activate."

violations.suggestion: alt="A smiling man with glasses in a professional office setting."
violations.explanation: WCAG 1.1.1 (Non-text Content) requires all `` elements to have an `alt` attribute. As this is an 'Illustrative Product Image,' it conveys meaningful content and is not purely decorative. The alt text provides a concise, equivalent description of the image's content for users of assistive technologies, such as screen readers, ensuring they receive the same information as sighted users.
violations.screen_reader_b: "A smiling man with glasses in a professional office setting.. Image."

violations.html: <input classname="flex-1 px-4 py-2 bg-slate-950 border border-slate-700 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-500" placeholder="Enter your corporate email address" type="email"/>
violations.explanation: The `aria-label` attribute provides a required accessible name for the input field, which screen readers announce to users. A `placeholder` alone is insufficient as it disappears on input and is not a reliable substitute for a label, failing WCAG 3.3.2 (Labels or Instructions) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
violations.patched_html: <input classname="flex-1 px-4 py-2 bg-slate-950 border border-slate-700 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-500" placeholder="Enter your corporate email address" type="email" aria-label="Corporate email address" />
violations.screen_reader_a: "Edit text. Placeholder: Enter your corporate email address"
violations.screen_reader_b: "Corporate email address. Edit text. Enter values."
violations.original_code: <input classname="flex-1 px-4 py-2 bg-slate-950 border border-slate-700 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-500" placeholder="Enter your corporate email address" type="email"/>
violations.remediated_code: <input classname="flex-1 px-4 py-2 bg-slate-950 border border-slate-700 rounded-md focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-indigo-500" placeholder="Enter your corporate email address" type="email" aria-label="Corporate email address" />

benchmarks.description: Sleek administrative operations console generated by AI. It features custom spans for interactive workspace addition and unlabelled SVG actions.

violations.html: Add Member
violations.suggestion: role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === "Enter" || e.key === " ") { e.preventDefault(); e.currentTarget.click(); } }}
violations.explanation: Using a non-interactive element like a `<span>` as a button is inaccessible. The fix adds `role="button"` to identify it as a button to assistive technologies (WCAG 4.1.2), `tabIndex={0}` to include it in the keyboard navigation order, and an `onKeyDown` handler to enable activation with the 'Enter' and 'Space' keys, mimicking native button behavior (WCAG 2.1.1).
violations.patched_html:
<span classname="inline-flex cursor-pointer items-center justify-center px-4 py-2 bg-indigo-600 hover:bg-indigo-700 text-white rounded font-medium text-sm transition" onclick="jsx-expression">
                        Add Member
                    </span role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === "Enter" || e.key === " ") { e.preventDefault(); e.currentTarget.click(); } }} >
violations.screen_reader_b: "[Focused]. Button. Press Space or Enter to activate."

violations.html:
<button classname="p-2 border border-slate-700 rounded bg-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-800 text-slate-300" onclick="jsx-expression">
<svg classname="w-5 h-5" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewbox="0 0 24 24">
<path d="M21 21l-6-6m2-5a7 7 0 11-14 0 7 7 0 0114 0z" strokelinecap="round" strokelinejoin="round" strokewidth="jsx-expression"></path>
</svg>
</button>
violations.explanation: Icon-only buttons require an accessible name for screen readers to announce their purpose. The `aria-label` attribute provides this name directly on the interactive button element, satisfying WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) and ensuring the control's function is understandable without visual cues.
violations.patched_html:
<button classname="p-2 border border-slate-700 rounded bg-slate-900 hover:bg-slate-800 text-slate-300" onclick="jsx-expression">
<svg classname="w-5 h-5" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" viewbox="0 0 24 24">
<path d="M21 21l-6-6m2-5a7 7 0 11-14 0 7 7 0 0114 0z" strokelinecap="round" strokelinejoin="round" strokewidth="jsx-expression"></path>
</svg>
</button aria-label="Search" >

violations.explanation: The button contains only an SVG icon, providing no text for screen readers. Adding an `aria-label` attribute gives the button an accessible name that describes its function (e.g., 'Settings'), which is announced by assistive technologies. This fulfills WCAG 1.1.1 (Non-text Content) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

benchmarks.description: Premium shopping grid layout generated by AI. Uses empty interactive links for favorite icons and image modules without alternate text labels.

violations.suggestion: role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); e.currentTarget.click(); } }}
violations.explanation: Using a 'div' as a button requires specific attributes to be accessible. The 'role="button"' attribute communicates its function to assistive technologies (WCAG 4.1.2). 'tabIndex={0}' makes the element focusable via keyboard. The 'onKeyDown' handler ensures it can be activated with standard keys ('Enter' or 'Space'), making it keyboard-operable as required by WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).
violations.patched_html: <div classname="w-5 h-5 rounded-full bg-indigo-600 cursor-pointer border border-slate-700 hover:ring-2 hover:ring-indigo-400 transition" onclick="jsx-expression"></div role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); e.currentTarget.click(); } }} >

violations.suggestion: role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); e.currentTarget.click(); } }}
violations.explanation: To make a `div` function as an accessible button, it requires `role='button'` to define its purpose for assistive technologies (WCAG 4.1.2), `tabIndex={0}` to be included in the keyboard focus order, and an `onKeyDown` handler to allow activation with 'Enter' and 'Space' keys. This meets WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard).

violations.suggestion: alt="A stainless steel watch with a black face resting on a reflective surface next to green leaves."
violations.explanation: WCAG 1.1.1 (Non-text Content) requires a text alternative for all informative images. The `alt` attribute provides a description of the product, ensuring that users of assistive technologies like screen readers can understand the image's content.
violations.screen_reader_b: "A stainless steel watch with a black face resting on a reflective surface next to green leaves.. Image."

violations.explanation: The link contains only an SVG icon, which is invisible to screen readers. Adding an `aria-label` provides a descriptive text alternative that screen readers can announce, explaining the link's purpose. This fulfills WCAG 2.4.4 (Link Purpose) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
